steps.title: Group factors by base
steps.content: Rewrite the product so like bases sit together: $(m^4 \cdot m^1)(q^4 \cdot q^5)(z^{-1} \cdot z^3)$.

steps.title: Add exponents for same bases
steps.content:
Using $a^m \cdot a^n = a^{m+n}$:
• $m$: $4 + 1 = 5$
• $q$: $4 + 5 = 9$
• $z$: $-1 + 3 = 2$
The product simplifies to $m^5 q^9 z^2$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A ($m^4 q^{20} z^{-3}$): multiplies the $q$ exponents and subtracts $z$ exponents instead of adding — wrong.
  • Choice B ($m^5 q^9 z^2$): exponents $5$, $9$, $2$ match the sums.
  • Choice C ($m^6 q^8 z^{-1}$): exponents off by $1$ in each variable — wrong.
  • Choice D ($m^{20} q^{12} z^{-2}$): multiplies exponents instead of adding — wrong.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
